description: DuckDuckGo [DONE] removal + X-Vqd-Hash-1 test, DeepSeek re-test

results.event_1_data: 0:"⚠ 이 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다..."
results.ui_response: 이런... Duck.ai를 일시적으로 사용할 수 없습니다. 잠시 후 다시 시도하세요.
results.screenshot: Generic error displayed, not warning text

services.analysis: CRITICAL FINDING: [DONE] removal did NOT fix the generic error. Root cause is ERR_INVALID_ACTION, not a parsing error. SSE events parse correctly (2 events: text-delta with warning text + finish with finishReason:stop). But DuckDuckGo client validates response 'actions' at app level — the Vercel AI SDK data format (0:"text", d:{...}) is parsed fine by the stream reader, but the app's business logic rejects the response because it doesn't contain a valid 'action' (likely expecting specific action types from the backend). Also: X-Vqd-Hash-1 response header is MISSING — this header is required by DuckDuckGo for session continuity and its absence may trigger additional validation failures. Next steps: (1) Add X-Vqd-Hash-1 header to block response, (2) Investigate what 'actions' the client expects in the SSE data — may need to wrap warning text in a specific action envelope.
DeepSeek session expired (same as #422). Cannot test h2_end_stream=2 without login. Repeated settings/list API calls visible in network (session polling). Need to re-authenticate to test.

summary.duckduckgo: FAILURE — [DONE] removal ineffective. Root cause: ERR_INVALID_ACTION (app-level action validation)
key_findings: DuckDuckGo error is NOT caused by [DONE] event — removing it changed nothing
Root cause is ERR_INVALID_ACTION: app validates response 'actions' beyond SSE format
SSE parsing works perfectly: 2 events correctly parsed (text-delta + finish)
X-Vqd-Hash-1 header is MISSING from block response — required for session continuity
time_to_first_token=178ms confirms fast response delivery (not a timeout issue)
Model detected as gpt-5-mini (DuckDuckGo's default model updated from previous)
next_investigation: Capture real DuckDuckGo chat response to see what 'actions' the client expects
Add X-Vqd-Hash-1 header to response (may need dummy hash value)
Check if DuckDuckGo wraps text in action objects like {type:'chat', content:...}
Consider: DuckDuckGo may use a custom protocol on top of Vercel AI SDK format
